sn45.15:1.2: This is also claimed elsewhere by the Buddha (DN 16:5.27.1). The path factors must come as a whole in order to be “right”, i.e. to lead to awakening. The Buddha is obviously not claiming that every aspect of the path is only found in Buddhism, but that the full range of qualities, and the exclusion of the wrong factors, is unique. For example, other religions may also teach moral behavior, but assert the existence of God or the efficacy of rituals.
